assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified test file has both additive and reductive changes; new test file is additive |'. Lines 85-98 provide detailed MIXED classification analysis covering both additive and reductive signals.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 87: '-1 test function removed (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)'. Line 92: 'The removed test (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`) tested qualifier-specific behavior that no longer exists.' This is explicitly identified as part of the reductive signals in the MIXED classification.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 91: 'Assertion specificity: the removed assertion checked a specific fully qualified PURL; the new assertions check for absence of qualifiers and match a simplified PURL -- this is a change in what is asserted, aligned with the new behavior'. Lines 89-90 also note '-1 assertion removed (fully qualified PURL comparison)' under the structural scan of the modified file.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 95-96: '**New file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`:** Purely additive: 3 new test functions, all new assertions, no removals.'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 88: '+1 test function added (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)' under the structural scan of the modified file `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs`. Line 92 also references it: 'The new test (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`) covers the replacement behavior (deduplication).'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only read-only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. The report is an analysis document that reads and evaluates the PR diff. No code files were created or modified. The report ends with '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]...*' indicating it is a review comment, not a code change.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings with a summary table, detailed analysis, and observations. The overall verdict is 'PASS' but no merge action is taken. The report includes non-blocking observations (lines 106-110) and ends as an AI-generated comment for review. There is no merge command, no auto-merge instruction, and no indication that the PR was merged.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md contains the Test Change Classification as a standalone row in the main verification table (line 14) and a standalone section (lines 85-98 titled '#### Test Change Classification -- MIXED'). There is no mention of a 'Style/Conventions sub-agent' or 'Style/Conventions domain' anywhere in the report or criterion files. The classification is not attributed to any sub-agent domain.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural scan (lines 86-91) does reference file content: function additions/removals and assertion changes. However, the semantic assessment (line 92) explicitly references the task specification: 'The removal is intentional and task-directed (Test Requirement #2 explicitly calls for it).' This violates constraint 1.18 by grounding the classification in acceptance criteria/task requirements rather than purely in test file content comparison. The classification verdict is informed by task requirements, not solely by base-branch vs PR-branch file content analysis.
